webrtc.enabled: Leak Protection (not affected by allow/block lists)

clientHints: `versions` should use the format `brand=version`, with multiple values separated by commas
`formFactors` supports multiple values separated by commas
languages: Custom: separate multiple values with commas, e.g. `en-US,en,zh-CN,zh`. The first value is treated as the preferred language.
desc.canvas: Canvas fingerprinting detects device traits by analyzing subtle differences in how graphics are drawn.
desc.audio: Audio fingerprinting identifies your device through unique patterns in audio signal processing.
desc.webgl: WebGL fingerprinting captures device characteristics based on variations in 3D rendering behavior.
webrtc: - System value: Keep the browser’s default WebRTC behavior without changes.

- Leak‑protection: Try to keep WebRTC working while hiding local or real IP addresses. If the browser cannot apply the policy, the extension will automatically switch to full disable. ***Note: If you choose this option, it will always take effect. Your allow or block list settings will not change it.***
- Disable: Turn off WebRTC completely, making all related features unavailable.
desc.font: Font fingerprinting reveals device details by checking which fonts are supported and how they’re rendered.
desc.webgpu: WebGPU fingerprinting extracts device information by analyzing differences in GPU processing capabilities.
desc.domRect: DOMRect fingerprinting gathers device data by measuring variations in element positioning and dimensions.
serviceWorker: When disabled, websites are prevented from registering Service Workers, blocking them from using Service Workers to collect device information.
When disabled, some websites may behave abnormally or fail to function properly.
desc.log-level: Only show logs at or above the selected level
desc.seed: Global Seed, Acts on **Random by Global Seed** Options
fast-inject: When enabled, a faster script injection method will be used, requiring browser support; when disabled, compatibility mode is applied.
- `Chrome 138+` Enable the **Allow running user scripts** option on the extension details page, no Developer Mode required
- `Edge 140+` Enable the **Allow user scripts** option on the extension details page, no Developer Mode required

webrtc.fallback-disabled: Effective state: Fell back to disabled

action.enable-fail: Failed to enable, check the "?" option description
action.input-hostname: Please enter a valid domain name

site-cleanup.scope-all-prompt: Reset this site? Note: resetting will log you out!
site-cleanup.scope-all-ok: Site reset complete, please refresh the page
site-cleanup.scope-cache-ok: Site cache cleared, please refresh the page

subscribe: When the extension loads, it will fetch configuration from the subscription target. It will overwrite the original settings and merge the site list. The configuration must be in JSON format. Refer to README.md for more details.
site-cleanup: Scope: Only affects the site on this page. Other subdomains under the same domain are not affected.
Clear cache: Use when page styles look broken, the site always loads old versions, cache issues occur, or the site behaves oddly but you don’t want to log out.
Reset site: Use when the site keeps remembering you, local data looks wrong, or you want to fully reset the site. **Warning: this will log you out!**
record: Record the call of fingerprint api, some api are often used when rendering elements, so the number of records for some api will be very large
Record the source address and number of iframe loaded
